description:
Spec bl-2e26 §13 ('bl prime — readiness, built FROM sync', the 'prime != install, but prime --install fuses them on demand' paragraph). NOT yet built: `run()` accepts only `--as` for prime (any other arg errors), and install is not wired into `run()` (it prints its Op plan) — so the orchestration glue has nothing to call yet.

WHY: `prime --install <center>` is the one-command first-contact / re-adopt. Plain prime is auto-safe (seeds the LOCAL default, syncs what landing already names, never adopts foreign config or activates third-party code). Adopting a center's config+plugins is the deliberate, consent-gated `install` (§6). The `--install` flag IS the consent.

WHAT (src/checkout.rs prime + flag parse): - `bl prime --install <center>`: (1) ensure local substrate (existing prime path), (2) `install` copies the center's config + plugin wiring into the landing (§6, consent-gated, scope-addressed — bl-3dd9), (3) a FINAL prime brings the just-adopted tasks_branch to readiness. - SINGLE hop, NOT a walk: a center's config is self-contained — it names its own tasks_branch (the one config->store indirection, §4), never another config to chase. The older RECURSIVE multi-hop form (the 1d-old note's 'prime->install->prime->install down the config chain') was a config-TRAIL artifact, RETIRED with config-shadowing (§4/§12 — config crosses a checkout boundary exactly ONCE). Do NOT build the recursive form. - Each install is idempotent, so a failed adopt RESUMES rather than double-applies. - prime never PUSHES config (outbound transfer is `install --to`, §6); plain prime still cannot activate code, so the auto-safe property of the every-session path holds.

DEPS: bl-3dd9 (install must be scope-addressed + wired/dispatched — this calls `install --from`), bl-cd21 (takes a <center> remote — needs the --remote>--center>XDG>origin resolution + the seeded-default-mismatch WARNING whose remedy text is literally 'run bl install / prime --install'). The pointer/trail-walk removal that prime --install's auto-safe framing rests on already landed (bl-9329, commit a9b476b).

issue.last_synced_title: Greenfield §13: bl prime --install <center> — single-hop fuse of prime + install --from + prime [bl-6e93]
